mission.title: kubeflow: crud-web-apps 1.3 Could not find CSRF cookie XSRF-TOKEN in the request
mission.description: crud-web-apps 1.3 Could not find CSRF cookie XSRF-TOKEN in the request. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
When trying to create a new notebook server, tensorboard in the tensorboards-web-app or PVC with the volumes web app it fails with the following error:
```
Could not find CSRF cookie XSRF-TOKEN in the request.

steps.title: Apply the fix for crud-web-apps 1.3 Could not find CSRF cookie XSRF-TOKEN in…
steps.description:
This is because by default the CSRF cookie will be set using the `Secure` attribute.

But in your case I see that you connect via plain http, which prevents the cookie to be set. Here's the relevant section from MDN https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Set-Cookie#Secure
> Note:
```yaml
Could not find CSRF cookie XSRF-TOKEN in the request. http://10.14.0.121/jupyter/api/namespaces/kubeflow-user/notebooks
```

steps.title: Confirm crud-web-apps 1.3 Could not find CSRF cookie… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This is because by default the CSRF cookie will be set using the `Secure` attribute.

But in your case I see that you connect via plain http, which prevents the cookie to be set.

  dnsNames:
    - kubeflow.example.com

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
